channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 8 arrested in Laos after 6 tourists died from suspected methanol poisoning
item.description: Officials arrested eight hostel workers in Laos on Monday for allegedly poisoning travelers with methanol, according to reports from the Australian Broadcasting Corp. Tourists reported illnesses after consuming alcohol in the town of Vang Vieng, a popular backpacking destination.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: New subway system in Greece puts archaeological finds discovered during construction on display for passengers
item.description: A new subway system is on its way to being showcased to passengers in Thessaloniki, Greece. The project unveiled thousands of ancient finds through excavations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia expels British diplomat after accusing him of spying
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian authorities on Tuesday ordered a British diplomat to leave the country on allegations of spying that the U.K. dismissed as “baseless” as tensions soar over the conflict in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine says Russian attack sets a new record for the number of drones used
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched 188 drones against most regions of Ukraine in a nighttime blitz, the Ukrainian air force said Tuesday, describing it as a record number of drones deployed in a single attack.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Protesters demanding Imran Khan's release march on Pakistan's capital. At least 6 people are killed
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Supporters seeking the release of imprisoned Pakistani former Prime Minister Imran Khan broke through a ring of shipping containers blocking off the capital on Tuesday, and battled security forces despite a government threat to respond with gunfire.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican cattle imports suspended after New World screwworm detected
item.description: The United States Department of Agriculture’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) announced that a New World screwworm (NWS) was detected in Mexico on Friday, causing the agency to pause animal imports from the country. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 6 dead as protests erupt in Pakistan over jailed former Prime Minister Imran Khan
item.description: Six people were killed as protests supporting imprisoned former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan escalated in Islamabad on Tuesday, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top EU diplomat says Israel has 'no excuses' to refuse a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — The European Union’s top diplomat said Tuesday there were “no excuses” for Israel to refuse to accept a ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, saying all its security concerns had been addressed in the U.S.-French-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bangladeshi court denies bail to Hindu leader who led rallies for minority protection
item.description: A prominent Bangladeshi Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation has been ordered detained on charges of sedition.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: French mass rape trial prosecutors demand maximum sentence for Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband
item.description: AVIGNON, France (AP) — A mammoth rape trial in France moved into a new phase Monday as prosecutors began to lay out the verdicts and punishments they want for dozens of men accused of raping Gisèle Pelicot while she was drugged and rendered unconscious by her husband.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and MARINE LESPRIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man charged in bomb hoax at US Embassy in London
item.description: A man was charged in connection with last week’s bomb hoax outside the U.S. Embassy in London, authorities from the U.K. said Monday. Daniel Parmenter, 43, was charged and taken into custody Monday, after appearing at Ealing Magistrates' Court. He was arrested at his home Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scuffles in Serbian parliament as deadly station collapse sparks anger at the government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Scuffles and fistfights broke out between ruling party and opposition lawmakers in Serbia’s parliament on Monday, weeks after a deadly rail station roof collapse that ignited tensions in the Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia tricks Yemeni men to fight in Ukraine under Houthi scheme
item.description: Russia has begun luring Yemeni men to fight in its war in Ukraine by falsely promising lucrative jobs before forcing them to sign military contracts and then sending them to fight on the front lines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Unidentified drones reported over RAF bases used by US
item.description: U.S. troops in Europe reported a swarm of unidentified drones flying over several Air Force bases in the U.K. in the past week, a sighting that comes amid a growing mystery of UFO sightings near military installations.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine to analyze fragments of missile fired by Russia capable of carrying nuclear warheads
item.description: Ukrainian investigators allowed journalists to view remains of an intermediate-range ballistic missile used by Russia on the battlefield, before an analysis could be completed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man in India regains consciousness before his cremation on funeral pyre: reports
item.description: A 25-year-old man who was declared dead in India regained consciousness shortly before he was set to be cremated this week, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Scientists study ‘very rare’ frozen remains of 35,000-year-old saber-toothed cub
item.description: Scientists studied a three-week-old saber-toothed cub dating back 35,000 that had been left almost perfectly intact, thanks to its frozen condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippine vice president makes public assassination threat against country's president
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said she has contracted a hired killer to assassinate her country's president in a bizarre public threat made amid worsening political divisions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel keeping its ‘eyes open’ for Iranian attacks during Trump transition period, ambassador says
item.description: Israel's Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon speaks to Fox News Digital about the threat from Iran during the Trump transition period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, WGN 9
